_meta.authored_from: §7.5 (Rust read-only access to iptv.db / server.db via sqlx)
_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
_meta.reviewed_by: ambitions-audit synthesis (Step 4: emerald-contracts crate; foot-gun F8 ANALYZE; F12 orphan link rows)
_meta.purpose: Lock the TEXT timestamp coercion and INTEGER 0/1 → bool coercion semantics for Rust sqlx readers of Hono-owned databases. Rust must round-trip these representations identically to better-sqlite3 + TS Date.toISOString().

scope.consumers: Rust media-core (M3) reads iptv.db and server.db read-only. Rust transcoder (M4) reads server.db read-only. Recommender (Python) reads exchange.db read-only.
scope.rust_pool_config: URI 'file:<path>?mode=ro'. NO PRAGMA writes. NEVER run sqlx::migrate! against these — Hono owns schema_migrations.

type_coercions.sql_column: TEXT (ISO-8601 datetime with 'T' separator and 'Z' suffix)
type_coercions.ts_target: Date (Date.toISOString() round-trips byte-identical when the 'Z' suffix is present)
edge_cases: Whitespace separator (SQLite datetime('now') default) MUST be accepted alongside the 'T' separator.
Fractional seconds (.124) MUST be accepted. Missing fractional seconds MUST be accepted.
'Z' suffix is canonical (UTC). Timezone-offset suffixes ('+00:00') are NOT used by Hono's writer; Rust readers SHOULD reject them rather than silently coerce.
INTEGER > 1 MUST be rejected as malformed (no Python-truthy coercion).
NULL → None in Rust Option<bool>; in TS the column is nullable iff the schema allows.

Always seconds — NEVER milliseconds for token-related fields. Hono always passes Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000); Rust SHOULD assert i64 is in a 'reasonable' range (e.g., > 1700000000 == 2023-11-15) when reading from columns named iat/nbf/exp.

table_specific_invariants.invariant: iptv_id has NO FK constraint to vod/series catalog tables. Rust MUST tolerate orphaned rows; query AND removed_at IS NULL alongside any tmdb_id lookup.
PK is (kind, category_id). category_id integer is NOT globally unique; channels/vod/series reference category_id with NO FK. Rust lookups MUST scope by kind.

applied_at TEXT NOT NULL in TS DDL; applied_at TEXT NULLABLE in Python DDL (divergence). Rust readers MUST tolerate NULL applied_at for backward compat. Rust-owned media.db schema_migrations DDL MUST lock applied_at TEXT NOT NULL.

rowid encoding: kind='movie' → rowid = tmdb_id; kind='tv' → rowid = tmdb_id | (1 << 32). This is in Python code only, not discoverable from DDL. Rust MUST replicate; document in code with `// matches recommender/app/db.py encode_vec_rowid`.

pragma_at_connect.read_only: Rust connects with URI '?mode=ro'. No pragma writes needed (WAL is set by Hono writer per-connection; readers inherit; foreign_keys not required for read-only).
pragma_at_connect.busy_timeout_note: Rust SHOULD set PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 to match Python (db.py) default and to avoid SQLITE_BUSY under writer-side commits. TS (better-sqlite3) does not set this today — known divergence.
